initial_draft:
<my name> Player BLUE </my name>
<MOVE> 1/4 </MOVE>
<my resources> Dollars: 0 </my resources>
<reason> I have no resources to trade, so I cannot accept this proposal. However, I can reject it, which would end the game immediately and result in both players losing everything. Since I have nothing to gain from accepting, I should reject to avoid losing anything. </reason>
<player answer> REJECT </player answer>
<message> I have no resources to accept your proposal. I will reject it to avoid losing anything. </message>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

iterations.feedback:
<critique>
1. Format: The response contains all required tags in the correct order, and the content is valid. No missing or malformed tags detected.

2. Payoff alignment: OK. Since Player BLUE has no resources and the game ends immediately upon rejection, there is no better split to propose — the outcome is the same regardless of the trade.

3. Opponent plausibility: OK. Since Player BLUE has no resources, the only plausible move is to reject, as accepting would not provide any benefit.

4. Consistency: OK. The <reason>, <player answer>, and <newly proposed trade> are all consistent with each other, and the <message> does not contradict the move.

5. Rule compliance: OK. The move respects the rules — Player BLUE is on move 1/4 and is not restricted from rejecting. The game ends immediately upon rejection, which is in line with the rules.
</critique>
